subject: Microsoft Azure
description: Microsoft Azure is a cloud computing platform offering a wide range of services including computing, analytics, storage, and networking. It provides infrastructure as a service (IaaS), platform as a service (PaaS), and software as a service (SaaS) capabilities for building, deploying, and managing applications through Microsoft's global network of data centers.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Azure?
questions.answer: Microsoft Azure is a cloud computing platform that provides infrastructure, platform, and software services through a global network of Microsoft-managed data centers. It enables organizations to build, deploy, and manage applications without investing in physical hardware. Azure supports various programming languages, frameworks, and tools, making it versatile for different workload requirements.
wrongAnswers: A social media platform for Microsoft products and services
A hardware manufacturing company specializing in server equipment
An open-source software development framework

questions.question: What is cloud computing?
questions.answer: Cloud computing is the delivery of computing services including servers, storage, databases, networking, software, and analytics over the internet (the cloud). It eliminates the need for upfront investment in physical infrastructure and allows organizations to pay only for resources they use. Cloud computing provides scalability, flexibility, and cost-efficiency compared to traditional on-premises infrastructure.
wrongAnswers: A type of weather forecasting technology
A network of physical servers located in different countries
Software installed on local computers for offline use

questions.question: What are the three main service models in cloud computing?
questions.answer: The three service models are: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) provides virtualized computing resources over the internet, PaaS (Platform as a Service) offers a platform for developing, running, and managing applications without infrastructure complexity, and SaaS (Software as a Service) delivers fully managed software applications over the internet on a subscription basis.
wrongAnswers: Public Cloud, Private Cloud, and Hybrid Cloud
Serverless, Container-based, and Virtual Machine services
Basic, Standard, and Premium service tiers

questions.question: What are the three types of cloud deployment models?
questions.answer: Public Cloud uses shared infrastructure operated by a third-party provider. Private Cloud is dedicated to a single organization with exclusive resources. Hybrid Cloud combines public and private clouds, allowing data and applications to be shared between them. Each model offers different benefits in terms of cost, security, and flexibility.
wrongAnswers: IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS service models
Local, Regional, and Global deployment options
Basic, Standard, and Enterprise tiers

questions.question: What is Azure Resource Manager (ARM)?
questions.answer: Azure Resource Manager is the deployment and management service for Azure. It provides a management layer that enables you to create, update, and delete resources in your Azure subscription. ARM supports role-based access control, tagging, and template-based deployments, making infrastructure management consistent and repeatable.
wrongAnswers: A physical hardware component in Azure data centers
A programming language for writing Azure applications
A database service for storing Azure resource metadata

questions.question: What are the benefits of Azure Resource Manager?
questions.answer: ARM benefits include: declarative template-based deployments, dependency management for correct resource ordering, access control through RBAC, tagging for resource organization, billing tracking, and consistent management across all Azure services. Templates can be version controlled and reused for consistent deployments.
wrongAnswers: Faster network speeds between Azure resources
Automatic backup of all Azure resources daily
Built-in antivirus protection for all VMs

questions.question: What is an Azure Resource Group?
questions.answer: A Resource Group is a container that holds related resources for an Azure solution. It acts as a logical container for organizing, deploying, managing, and monitoring resources together. All resources in a group should share the same lifecycle, making it easier to deploy, update, and delete resources as a single unit.
wrongAnswers: A physical location where Azure servers are housed
A billing unit for Azure subscription charges
A security group for managing user access

questions.question: What is Azure Virtual Network (VNet)?
questions.answer: Azure Virtual Network enables Azure resources to securely communicate with each other, the internet, and on-premises networks. It provides isolation, segmentation, and connectivity capabilities including subnets, network security groups, and VPN gateways. VNet is fundamental for building secure cloud architectures.
wrongAnswers: A virtual machine running network monitoring software
A physical network switch in Azure data centers
A VPN client application for Azure users

questions.question: What is a subnet in Azure?
questions.answer: A subnet is a range of IP addresses within a VNet that logically divides the network. Subnets allow you to segment the network and apply security policies. Best practice is to create separate subnets for different tiers (web, application, data) and use Network Security Groups to control traffic between them.
wrongAnswers: A physical cable connecting servers in a rack
A subnet mask used for IP address calculation
A type of Azure storage account

questions.question: What is Azure Virtual Machine (VM)?
questions.answer: Azure Virtual Machine is an IaaS resource that provides on-demand, scalable computing capacity. VMs support various operating systems including Windows and Linux. They can be customized with different sizes, storage options, and configurations. Azure VMs are suitable for lift-and-shift migrations, development environments, and custom workloads.
wrongAnswers: A physical server purchased from Microsoft
A container running in Azure Container Instances
A serverless function in Azure Functions

questions.question: What are the different Azure VM sizes?
questions.answer: Azure offers VM sizes in series: Basic (A series) for dev/test, Standard (D series) for general workloads, Memory optimized (E series, M series) for database workloads, Compute optimized (F series), and GPU-enabled (N series) for AI and graphics. Each series offers different vCPU, memory, and storage configurations.
wrongAnswers: Small, Medium, Large, and Extra Large sizes
Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 enterprise grades
Development, Testing, and Production tiers

questions.question: What is Azure Availability Set?
questions.answer: Availability Set is a logical grouping of VMs that ensures applications remain available during planned and unplanned maintenance. It distributes VMs across fault domains (separate power, network) and update domains (reboot groups). This ensures at least one VM remains operational during maintenance events.
wrongAnswers: A set of VMs with identical configurations for load balancing
A collection of backup copies of VMs
A group of VMs that automatically scale together

questions.question: What is Azure Availability Zone?
questions.answer: Availability Zones are physically separate locations within an Azure region, each with independent power, cooling, and networking. They protect against datacenter failures and provide higher availability than availability sets alone. Zones are connected with low-latency links for replication and redundancy.
wrongAnswers: Geographic regions where Azure has data centers
Network zones for firewall rule configuration
Security zones for different trust levels

questions.question: What is Azure Scale Set?
questions.answer: Virtual Machine Scale Set automatically increases or decreases VM capacity based on demand. It allows you to deploy and manage identical VMs, automatically adjusting capacity based on metrics like CPU usage. Scale Sets support load balancing, health probes, and automatic upgrades for zero-downtime updates.
wrongAnswers: A set of tools for measuring VM performance
A billing unit for VM resource consumption
A collection of VM backup policies

questions.question: What is Azure Load Balancer?
questions.answer: Azure Load Balancer distributes incoming network traffic across multiple VMs to ensure no single server becomes overwhelmed. It operates at Layer 4 (TCP/UDP) and supports health probes for detecting unavailable instances. Basic SKU is for simple scenarios, Standard SKU offers advanced features and SLA.
wrongAnswers: A physical hardware device for traffic distribution
A service for balancing Azure subscription costs
A tool for balancing CPU usage across cores

questions.question: What is Azure Application Gateway?
questions.answer: Application Gateway is a Layer 7 load balancer with web application firewall (WAF) capabilities. It routes traffic based on URL path, supports SSL termination, and provides features like cookie-based session affinity, URL routing, and multi-site hosting. Ideal for web applications requiring advanced routing and security.
wrongAnswers: A gateway application for connecting to Azure services
A physical network gateway device
A tool for monitoring application performance

questions.question: What is Azure Blob Storage?
questions.answer: Azure Blob Storage is an object storage solution for unstructured data. It supports three blob types: Block blobs for text/binary files, Page blobs for frequent read/write (like VHD), and Append blobs optimized for log data. Blob storage is highly scalable, durable, and accessible via REST API or SDKs.
wrongAnswers: A database for storing binary large objects
A virtual machine image storage service
A network protocol for data transfer

questions.question: What are Azure Storage Account types?
questions.answer: Storage account types: Standard (HDD-based for blobs, tables, queues, files), Premium (SSD-based for page blobs with high IOPS requirements). Within Standard, performance tiers include General Purpose v2 (recommended) and Blob Storage (legacy). Each has different pricing and capability profiles.
wrongAnswers: Basic, Standard, and Premium storage tiers
Hot, Cool, and Archive access tiers
Local, Regional, and Global storage accounts

questions.question: What is Azure File Storage?
questions.answer: Azure Files provides fully managed file shares in the cloud accessible via SMB protocol. Shares can be mounted concurrently on cloud or on-premises Windows, Linux, and macOS systems. Ideal for lift-and-shift of file shares, storing configuration files, and sharing data between applications.
wrongAnswers: A file transfer service for uploading data to Azure
A database for storing file metadata
A content delivery network for static files

questions.question: What is Azure Queue Storage?
questions.answer: Azure Queue Storage provides reliable messaging between application components. It stores many small messages (up to 64KB each) that can be accessed via authenticated HTTP/HTTPS calls. Queues enable asynchronous task processing and decouple components for scalability and reliability.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing print job queues
A load balancer for network traffic
A database for storing message history

questions.question: What is Azure Table Storage?
questions.answer: Azure Table Storage is a NoSQL key-value store for semi-structured data. It provides fast, cost-effective storage with automatic scaling. Tables store entities with properties, accessed via OData protocol or REST. Suitable for flexible schemas, logs, and non-relational data storage.
wrongAnswers: A spreadsheet service for Azure applications
A database for storing Azure resource configurations
A service for managing database table schemas

questions.question: What are Azure Storage redundancy options?
questions.answer: Redundancy options: LRS (Locally Redundant) - 3 copies in single datacenter, ZRS (Zone Redundant) - 3 copies across availability zones, GRS (Geo-Redundant) - 3 copies locally + 3 in paired region, GZRS (Geo-Zone Redundant) - ZRS locally + GRS in secondary. Choose based on durability requirements and cost.
wrongAnswers: Single, Double, and Triple redundancy levels
Bronze, Silver, and Gold redundancy tiers
Basic, Advanced, and Enterprise redundancy options

questions.question: What is Azure SQL Database?
questions.answer: Azure SQL Database is a fully managed PaaS database engine based on SQL Server. It handles database management functions like patching, backups, and monitoring without user intervention. Supports elastic pools for multi-tenant scenarios, built-in intelligence for performance optimization, and familiar SQL Server tooling.
wrongAnswers: A virtual machine running SQL Server software
A NoSQL document database service
A data warehouse for big data analytics

questions.question: What are Azure SQL Database deployment options?
questions.answer: Deployment options: Single Database for individual databases with dedicated resources, Elastic Pool for multiple databases sharing resources and cost efficiency, Managed Instance for near-complete SQL Server compatibility with PaaS benefits. Choose based on migration needs and existing application requirements.
wrongAnswers: Basic, Standard, and Premium database tiers
Development, Testing, and Production environments
Regional and Global deployment options

questions.question: What is Azure Cosmos DB?
questions.answer: Azure Cosmos DB is a globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database service. It supports multiple data models (document, key-value, graph, column-family) with turnkey global distribution. Offers guaranteed low latency, high availability, and consistency options from strong to eventual. Ideal for web, mobile, gaming, and IoT applications.
wrongAnswers: A SQL database service for Cosmos spacecraft data
A time series database for monitoring data
A graph database only service

questions.question: What is Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)?
questions.answer: Azure Active Directory is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service. It provides SSO (Single Sign-On) to thousands of SaaS applications, protects user credentials, enables conditional access policies, and supports multi-factor authentication. Azure AD is the foundation for Azure's security and access management.
wrongAnswers: A database service for storing active directory entries
A network monitoring tool for Azure resources
A file sharing service for enterprise collaboration

questions.question: What is Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) in Azure?
questions.answer: RBAC is an authorization system that provides fine-grained access management to Azure resources. It assigns permissions to security principals (users, groups, service principals) based on roles. Built-in roles like Owner, Contributor, Reader provide predefined permission sets, or create custom roles for specific needs.
wrongAnswers: A system for managing user passwords and credentials
A network protocol for secure data transfer
A database schema for access logs

questions.question: What is Azure Network Security Group (NSG)?
questions.answer: NSG filters network traffic to and from Azure resources in a VNet. It contains security rules allowing or denying inbound/outbound traffic based on source/destination IP, port, and protocol. NSGs can be associated with subnets or network interfaces to control traffic flow at Layer 3/4.
wrongAnswers: A group of VMs sharing the same network configuration
A physical firewall device in Azure data centers
A service for monitoring network traffic

questions.question: What is Azure VPN Gateway?
questions.answer: VPN Gateway connects Azure VNets to on-premises networks via Site-to-Site VPN, or connects individual devices via Point-to-Site VPN. It uses IPsec/IKE protocols for encrypted communication. VPN Gateway supports BGP routing for dynamic routing and can be deployed in active-active mode for redundancy.
wrongAnswers: A client application for connecting to Azure
A service for creating virtual private networks in VMs
A gateway for routing between Azure regions

questions.question: What is Azure ExpressRoute?
questions.answer: ExpressRoute provides dedicated private connectivity from on-premises to Microsoft cloud services without traversing the public internet. It offers higher reliability, lower latency, and stronger security than VPN connections. ExpressRoute Global Reach connects Microsoft clouds across regions.
wrongAnswers: A fast CDN service for static content delivery
A direct connection to Azure storage accounts
A premium support plan for Azure services

questions.question: What is Azure Content Delivery Network (CDN)?
questions.answer: Azure CDN caches static web content at strategically placed servers worldwide, delivering content to users from the nearest edge location. It reduces latency, decreases server load, and improves user experience. CDN integrates with Azure Blob Storage, Web Apps, and media services.
wrongAnswers: A service for delivering Azure billing reports
A network monitoring tool for Azure resources
A database for storing content metadata

questions.question: What is Azure DNS?
questions.answer: Azure DNS is a hosting service for DNS domains, providing name resolution using Azure infrastructure. It offers high availability, automatic SLA, and easy management alongside other Azure services. Supports alias records for Azure resources and DNSSEC for additional security.
wrongAnswers: A database for storing DNS zone configurations
A network monitoring tool for DNS servers
A service for managing domain registrations

questions.question: What is Azure Traffic Manager?
questions.answer: Traffic Manager is a DNS-based load balancer that distributes traffic across global Azure regions. It routes users to the best endpoint based on routing method (performance, priority, weighted, geographic). Traffic Manager improves application availability and performance without deploying additional infrastructure.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing network traffic rules
A tool for monitoring Azure network performance
A database for storing traffic statistics

questions.question: What is Azure App Service?
questions.answer: Azure App Service is a fully managed PaaS for web, mobile, and API applications. It supports .NET, Java, Node.js, Python, and PHP. Features include auto-scaling, deployment slots, SSL certificates, and integration with CI/CD pipelines. App Service handles infrastructure management automatically.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing application permissions
A database for storing application data
A monitoring tool for application performance

questions.question: What are Azure App Service deployment slots?
questions.answer: Deployment slots allow staging environments for web apps. You can swap slots to deploy to production with no downtime. Slots have their own configurations and allow testing before production deployment. This enables zero-downtime deployments and staging validation workflows.
wrongAnswers: Physical server slots in Azure data centers
Deployment roles for different team members
Storage slots for application assets

questions.question: What is Azure Functions?
questions.answer: Azure Functions is a serverless compute service that executes code in response to events without managing infrastructure. Supports multiple trigger types (HTTP, timer, blob, queue) and allows pay-per-execution pricing. Functions auto-scale based on load and integrate with various Azure services.
wrongAnswers: A service for creating HTTP endpoints for APIs
A background processing service for long-running tasks
A container orchestration service

questions.question: What is Azure Logic Apps?
questions.answer: Logic Apps is a serverless workflow automation service that connects apps and orchestrates business processes. It provides a visual designer for building workflows using connectors to Azure services, Microsoft products, and third-party APIs. Ideal for enterprise integration and B2B scenarios.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing application logic in databases
A tool for debugging application code
A container service for running logic apps

questions.question: What is Azure Service Bus?
questions.answer: Azure Service Bus is an enterprise message broker supporting publish-subscribe and queue-based messaging. It provides reliable async communication between applications and services, even across organizations. Supports transactions, dead-letter queues, and sessions for complex messaging patterns.
wrongAnswers: A bus service for connecting Azure regions
A network monitoring tool for Azure services
A database for storing service metadata

questions.question: What is Azure Event Hub?
questions.answer: Event Hub is a big data streaming platform capable of ingesting millions of events per second. It provides low-latency, real-time data ingestion from applications, devices, and sensors. Often used for telemetry, log aggregation, and real-time analytics pipelines.
wrongAnswers: A hub service for connecting IoT devices
A network routing service for events
A database for storing event history

questions.question: What is Azure Event Grid?
questions.answer: Event Grid is an event routing service that enables reactive programming. It distributes events from sources to handlers using pub-sub pattern. Supports filtering, routing, and retry policies for reliable event delivery. Integrates with Azure Functions and Logic Apps for event-driven processing.
wrongAnswers: A grid computing service for parallel processing
A network visualization tool for Azure resources
A database for storing grid data

questions.question: What is Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)?
questions.answer: AKS is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies container orchestration. It handles scaling, upgrades, and health monitoring automatically. AKS reduces operational overhead while providing access to advanced features like Azure AD integration, monitoring, and networking integrations.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing Kubernetes clusters on-premises
A database service for Kubernetes metadata
A monitoring tool for Kubernetes workloads

questions.question: What is Azure Container Instances (ACI)?
questions.answer: ACI provides serverless container hosting without infrastructure management. Deploy containers in seconds with a single command or API call. Ideal for burst workloads, CI/CD agents, or tasks that don't require a full Kubernetes cluster. Supports Windows and Linux containers.
wrongAnswers: A service for creating container images
A database for storing container metadata
A network service for container communication

questions.question: What is Azure Container Registry?
questions.answer: Container Registry is a managed private Docker registry service for storing and managing container images. It integrates with AKS and ACI for automated builds and deployments. Supports geo-replication, vulnerability scanning, and Helm chart storage.
wrongAnswers: A database for storing container logs
A network service for container networking
A monitoring tool for container health

questions.question: What is Azure Monitor?
questions.answer: Azure Monitor is a comprehensive monitoring solution for collecting, analyzing, and acting on telemetry from cloud and on-premises environments. It provides insights through metrics, logs, and application diagnostics. Integrates with Log Analytics, Application Insights, and various alerting solutions.
wrongAnswers: A network monitoring tool for Azure infrastructure
A service for monitoring Azure costs
A database for storing monitoring data

questions.question: What is Azure Application Insights?
questions.answer: Application Insights is an Application Performance Monitoring (APM) service for web applications. It automatically detects performance anomalies, provides powerful analytics tools, and helps identify issues. Supports .NET, Java, Node.js, Python, and JavaScript applications.
wrongAnswers: A service for testing application performance
A database for storing application logs
A tool for managing application deployments

questions.question: What is Azure Log Analytics?
questions.answer: Log Analytics is a tool in Azure Monitor for writing log queries and analyzing collected data. It uses Kusto Query Language (KQL) for powerful data exploration. Log Analytics aggregates logs from multiple sources including Azure resources, on-premises systems, and third-party services.
wrongAnswers: A database service for storing log files
A service for analyzing network logs
A tool for debugging application logs

questions.question: What is Azure Advisor?
questions.answer: Azure Advisor analyzes resource configurations and usage patterns to provide personalized recommendations. It covers cost optimization, security, reliability, operational excellence, and performance. Recommendations are generated automatically based on Azure best practices and are accessible from the Azure portal.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing Azure support tickets
A database for storing advisor recommendations
A tool for consulting with Azure experts

questions.question: What is Azure Service Health?
questions.answer: Service Health provides personalized alerts and guidance when Azure service issues affect you. It includes Service issues, planned maintenance, and health advisories. Service Health tracks your resources and notifies you of impacts affecting your specific Azure services and regions.
wrongAnswers: A service for monitoring VM health
A database for storing service health data
A tool for checking Azure subscription status

questions.question: What is Azure Resource Lock?
questions.answer: Resource Lock prevents accidental deletion or modification of resources. Two types: ReadOnly locks restrict all changes, Delete locks prevent deletion only. Locks are inherited from resource groups and can be applied at any level. Use locks on critical resources as a safety measure.
wrongAnswers: A physical lock for securing Azure data centers
A network security service for blocking traffic
A database for storing resource access logs

questions.question: What is Azure Policy?
questions.answer: Azure Policy enforces organizational standards and assesses compliance at scale. Policies can audit, enforce, or modify resource properties during deployment. Built-in policies cover common requirements, or create custom policies using JSON definitions. Policies evaluate resources and report compliance status.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing Azure support policies
A database for storing policy documents
A network policy service for traffic rules

questions.question: What is Azure Managed Identity?
questions.answer: Managed Identity eliminates the need to manage credentials by providing automatic identities for Azure resources. System-assigned identity is tied to resource lifecycle, while user-assigned identity is a standalone resource. Applications use identity to authenticate to Azure services without storing credentials.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing user identities manually
A database for storing identity information
A tool for monitoring identity access

questions.question: What is Azure Key Vault?
questions.answer: Azure Key Vault securely stores and manages sensitive information including secrets, keys, and certificates. It provides centralized key management with hardware security module (HSM) protection. Key Vault integrates with Azure services and supports access policies, firewall rules, and soft delete for data protection.
wrongAnswers: A database for storing encrypted data
A network service for securing connections
A tool for managing cryptographic keys manually

questions.question: What is Azure Bastion?
questions.answer: Azure Bastion provides secure RDP and SSH access to VMs without exposing public IPs. It deploys within your VNet and uses HTML5-based web client for access. Bastion eliminates the need for public IPs, NSG rules for management ports, and provides JIT access capabilities.
wrongAnswers: A physical secure room in Azure data centers
A service for managing bastion hosts
A database for storing secure connection logs

questions.question: What is Azure Firewall?
questions.answer: Azure Firewall is a managed network security service that protects Azure VNet resources. It provides FQDN filtering, network traffic filtering rules, and threat intelligence-based filtering. Firewall supports high availability, availability zones, and integrates with Azure Monitor for logging.
wrongAnswers: A physical firewall device in Azure data centers
A service for managing firewall rules
A database for storing firewall logs

questions.question: What is Azure DDoS Protection?
questions.answer: DDoS Protection provides defense against distributed denial of service attacks. Basic tier is automatically enabled for all Azure services. Standard tier adds tuned protection policies, attack metrics, and alerting for additional cost. DDoS Protection mitigates volumetric, protocol, and application layer attacks.
wrongAnswers: A service for protecting Azure databases
A network monitoring tool for detecting attacks
A database for storing attack information

questions.question: What is Azure Web Application Firewall (WAF)?
questions.answer: WAF provides centralized protection for web applications against common exploits and vulnerabilities. It implements OWASP Core Rule Sets for protection against SQL injection, XSS, and other attacks. WAF can be deployed with Application Gateway or Front Door for global protection.
wrongAnswers: A physical firewall for web servers
A service for managing web application security
A database for storing web security logs

questions.question: What is Azure Identity Protection?
questions.answer: Identity Protection provides risk detection and remediation for compromised identities. It uses machine learning to detect anomalies and risky sign-in behaviors. Policies can enforce MFA, block access, or require password changes based on risk level to protect accounts.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing user identities
A database for storing identity data
A monitoring tool for identity access

questions.question: What is Azure Conditional Access?
questions.answer: Conditional Access allows organizations to control access based on signals like user, location, device state, and risk. Policies can grant access, require MFA, or block access based on conditions. It's the tool for implementing zero-trust security by validating access requests.
wrongAnswers: A service for conditionally deploying resources
A database for storing access policies
A network service for conditional routing

questions.question: What is Azure Backup?
questions.answer: Azure Backup provides simple, secure, and cost-effective solutions for backing up data to Microsoft Cloud. It supports VMs, files, folders, databases (SQL, SAP), and Azure Files. Backup data is stored in Recovery Services vault with encryption and retention policies.
wrongAnswers: A service for backing up network configurations
A database for storing backup metadata
A tool for managing backup schedules

questions.question: What is Azure Site Recovery?
questions.answer: Site Recovery provides business continuity by orchestrating replication and failover of workloads. It supports Azure VMs, on-premises VMs, and physical servers for disaster recovery. Site Recovery handles test failovers, planned failovers, and unplanned failovers with minimal data loss.
wrongAnswers: A service for recovering accidentally deleted resources
A database for storing recovery points
A tool for managing disaster recovery plans

questions.question: What is Azure Recovery Services vault?
questions.answer: Recovery Services vault is a storage entity that houses backup data and recovery points over time. It stores backup data for Azure VMs, SQL in Azure VMs, file shares, and on-premises servers. Vaults support cross-region redundancy and role-based access control.
wrongAnswers: A physical vault in Azure data centers
A service for managing vault access
A database for storing vault configurations

questions.question: What is Azure Automation?
questions.answer: Azure Automation provides capabilities for automating cloud management tasks. It includes Desired State Configuration (DSC), runbooks for process automation, and update management. Automation reduces manual errors and enables consistent operations at scale.
wrongAnswers: A service for automating container deployments
A database for storing automation scripts
A tool for testing automation workflows

questions.question: What is Azure Update Management?
questions.answer: Update Management assesses operating system updates and manages update deployment for Windows and Linux. It identifies missing updates, schedules deployment during maintenance windows, and provides compliance reporting. Supports hybrid environments with Azure Arc-enabled servers.
wrongAnswers: A service for updating Azure resource configurations
A database for storing update history
A tool for managing software updates manually

questions.question: What is Azure Arc?
questions.answer: Azure Arc extends Azure management and services to any infrastructure including on-premises, edge, and multi-cloud. It provides unified management experience for servers, Kubernetes clusters, and data services. Arc enables Azure policies, monitoring, and security across environments.
wrongAnswers: A physical arc-shaped data center structure
A service for connecting Azure regions
A database for storing arc configurations

questions.question: What is Azure DevOps?
questions.answer: Azure DevOps provides developer services for CI/CD, version control, agile planning, and package management. Services include Azure Repos for Git hosting, Azure Pipelines for CI/CD, Azure Boards for work tracking, and Azure Artifacts for package hosting.
wrongAnswers: A service for developing Azure resources
A database for storing DevOps metrics
A tool for managing Azure development teams

questions.question: What is Azure Pipelines?
questions.answer: Azure Pipelines provides CI/CD for any language, platform, and cloud. It supports Git repositories, container registries, and deployment to multiple environments. Pipelines can be defined as YAML or built using visual designer, with built-in tasks for common deployment scenarios.
wrongAnswers: A physical pipeline for transporting data
A database for storing pipeline configurations
A service for managing pipeline connections

questions.question: What is Azure Cognitive Services?
questions.answer: Cognitive Services provides AI capabilities through pre-built APIs. Categories include Vision (image analysis), Speech (transcription, translation), Language (text analytics, translation), Decision (anomaly detector, content moderator), and Open AI (GPT models). No ML expertise required.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing cognitive abilities
A database for storing cognitive data
A tool for testing AI models

questions.question: What is Azure Machine Learning?
questions.answer: Azure Machine Learning is a PaaS service for building, training, and deploying ML models. It provides automated ML, visual designers, Python SDK, and MLOps capabilities. Supports frameworks like PyTorch, TensorFlow, and scikit-learn with enterprise security.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing ML model licenses
A database for storing ML training data
A tool for manual machine learning tasks

questions.question: What is Azure Bot Service?
questions.answer: Azure Bot Service provides tools for building, testing, and deploying intelligent bots. It supports integration with channels like Teams, Slack, and web chat. Bot Framework Composer offers visual authoring, while QnA Maker enables knowledge base-based responses.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing robot hardware
A database for storing bot conversations
A tool for debugging bot applications

questions.question: What is Azure IoT Hub?
questions.answer: IoT Hub provides managed service for bi-directional communication between IoT devices and Azure. It handles device identity, message routing, device twin, and monitoring. IoT Hub scales to millions of devices and integrates with Event Grid, Functions, and Stream Analytics.
wrongAnswers: A physical hub for connecting IoT devices
A service for managing IoT device passwords
A database for storing IoT telemetry data

questions.question: What is Azure IoT Central?
questions.answer: IoT Central is a SaaS solution for IoT device management without deep technical expertise. It provides pre-built dashboards, device templates, and rules. Connect devices using industry protocols, and integrate with business applications through built-in export capabilities.
wrongAnswers: A physical data center in the central region
A service for managing central IoT hubs
A database for storing central IoT data

questions.question: What is Azure Stream Analytics?
questions.answer: Stream Analytics is a real-time stream processing engine. It ingests data from IoT Hub, Event Hub, or Blob Storage, processes it using SQL-like queries, and outputs to sinks like SQL Database, Data Lake, or Power BI. Supports windowing functions for time-based aggregations.
wrongAnswers: A service for streaming video content
A database for storing stream metadata
A tool for analyzing network streams

questions.question: What is Azure Data Factory?
questions.answer: Data Factory is a hybrid data integration service for orchestrating data movement and transformation. It provides 90+ connectors to cloud and on-premises sources. Activities include copy, data flow, and HDInsight transformations. Pipelines can be triggered on schedule or events.
wrongAnswers: A factory for manufacturing Azure resources
A database for storing factory data
A service for managing data pipelines manually

questions.question: What is Azure Synapse Analytics?
questions.answer: Azure Synapse is an enterprise analytics service that combines data integration, data warehousing, and big data analytics. It provides SQL-based analytics with unlimited scale, Spark for big data processing, and integration with Power BI. Supports data exploration and real-time analytics.
wrongAnswers: A service for analyzing Azure subscription costs
A database for storing analytics data
A tool for synchronizing analytics across regions

questions.question: What is Azure Databricks?
questions.answer: Azure Databricks provides a fast, easy, collaborative Apache Spark-based analytics platform. It integrates with Azure data services for simplified data ingestion. Databricks offers auto-scaling clusters, notebook collaboration, and optimized Delta Lake for data engineering and ML workloads.
wrongAnswers: A physical brick data center structure
A service for managing brick-and-mortar stores
A database for storing brick data

questions.question: What is Azure Cost Management?
questions.answer: Azure Cost Management provides tools for monitoring, allocating, and optimizing cloud costs. It offers budget alerts, cost analysis, recommendations, and exports. Enable Cost Management alerts to notify stakeholders when spending exceeds thresholds.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing Azure subscription payments
A database for storing cost data
A tool for calculating resource costs manually

questions.question: How do you secure Azure SQL Database?
questions.answer: Secure SQL Database using: firewall rules for IP access, Azure AD authentication for identity management, TLS for encrypted connections, Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for data encryption, Dynamic Data Masking for sensitive data protection, and Always Encrypted for column-level encryption.
wrongAnswers: Disable all network access to the database
Store database credentials in plain text configuration
Use the same password for all database users

questions.question: What is Azure Private Link?
questions.answer: Private Link provides private connectivity from VNet to Azure services without traversing the internet. It creates a private endpoint that maps to the service, ensuring traffic stays on Azure backbone. Private Link eliminates data exposure to public internet and enables hybrid consistency.
wrongAnswers: A private network link between Azure regions
A service for linking private networks
A database for storing private connection data

questions.question: What is Azure Service Endpoint?
questions.answer: Service Endpoints enable private IP address connectivity from VNet to Azure services over Azure backbone. They secure service resources to VNet and provide optimal routing. Unlike Private Link, traffic still traverses Azure network but not public internet.
wrongAnswers: A service for creating endpoints for applications
A database for storing endpoint configurations
A tool for testing service endpoints

questions.question: What is Azure VNet Peering?
questions.answer: VNet Peering connects two VNets enabling resources to communicate using private IP addresses. Types include local peering (same region) and global peering (cross-region). Peered VNets appear as one network for connectivity, with no gateway transit by default.
wrongAnswers: A physical peer-to-peer connection between Azure regions
A service for managing peer identities
A database for storing peering configurations

questions.question: What is Azure Virtual WAN?
questions.answer: Virtual WAN is a networking service that simplifies branch connectivity to Azure. It provides SD-WAN capabilities, VPN connectivity, ExpressRoute integration, and transit connectivity between sites. Virtual WAN hub manages connectivity at scale with built-in security policies.
wrongAnswers: A wide area network for Azure regions only
A service for managing WAN connections manually
A database for storing WAN configurations

questions.question: What is Azure Gateway Transit?
questions.answer: Gateway Transit allows a VNet with VPN/ExpressRoute gateway to share its connection with peered VNets. The peer VNet can use the gateway for cross-premises connectivity without deploying its own gateway. One gateway can serve multiple peer VNets efficiently.
wrongAnswers: A transit service for routing between gateways
A database for storing transit configurations
A tool for managing gateway connections

questions.question: How do you troubleshoot VM connectivity issues in Azure?
questions.answer: Troubleshoot with: verify NIC configuration and IP assignment, check NSG rules for blocking traffic, review UDR if custom routing exists, test with Network Watcher IP flow check, verify VM firewall settings, ensure service listening on correct port, and check for SNAT port exhaustion.
wrongAnswers: Restart the Azure subscription to fix connectivity
Delete and recreate the VM to resolve issues
Disable all security features to test connectivity

questions.question: How do you troubleshoot RDP connections to Azure VMs?
questions.answer: Fix RDP issues by: resetting remote desktop configuration, checking NSG rules and endpoints, reviewing console logs, resetting the NIC, checking Resource Health, resetting password, restarting VM, or redeploying VM. Use Serial Console for deeper debugging.
wrongAnswers: Reinstall the operating system to fix RDP
Disable the Windows Firewall completely
Use a different RDP port without checking security

questions.question: What are the high-level requirements for Site-to-Site VPN?
questions.answer: Requirements: create VPN Gateway with S2S VPN type, create Local Network Gateway representing on-premises router, configure connection with public IP and pre-shared key, ensure compatible VPN device on-premises, configure BGP if dynamic routing needed.
wrongAnswers: Deploy a physical VPN device in Azure
Use the same private IP range on both sides
Disable all firewall rules between networks

questions.question: What is Azure SLA for Virtual Machines?
questions.answer: SLA depends on deployment: single VM provides 99.9% availability with premium managed disks. VMs in Availability Set with two+ instances per zone get 99.95% availability. Availability Zones with two+ zones get 99.99% availability guarantee.
wrongAnswers: All VMs have 99.99% SLA regardless of configuration
VMs have 99% SLA by default
SLA is only provided for premium VMs

questions.question: What is the difference between Standard SSD and Premium SSD?
questions.answer: Premium SSD offers better performance with very low latency, high IOPS/throughput, and consistent performance. Standard SSD provides cost-effective option for light workloads. Premium SSD is recommended for production workloads requiring consistent low latency.
wrongAnswers: Standard SSD is faster than Premium SSD
Premium SSD has lower durability than Standard SSD
Standard SSD is only for archival data

questions.question: What is Azure Spot VM?
questions.answer: Spot VM uses spare Azure capacity at significant cost savings (up to 90%). Azure can evict Spot VMs when capacity is needed. Suitable for batch processing, dev/test environments, and fault-tolerant workloads. Not recommended for production without proper handling.
wrongAnswers: A premium VM for high-priority workloads
A VM that runs in a specific Azure region
A VM with guaranteed uninterrupted service

questions.question: What is Azure Reserved Instance?
questions.answer: Reserved Instances (RIs) commit to 1 or 3 year terms for significant discounts (up to 72%) compared to pay-as-you-go pricing. RIs apply to VMs, SQL Database, Cosmos DB, and other resources. Benefits apply automatically to matching usage.
wrongAnswers: A physical server reserved for exclusive use
A VM with reserved IP addresses
A storage account with reserved capacity

questions.question: What is Azure Hybrid Benefit?
questions.answer: Hybrid Benefit allows using existing Windows Server and SQL Server licenses with Software Assurance for discounted pricing. One license can be reused on-premises and in Azure. Significantly reduces costs for organizations with existing Microsoft licenses.
wrongAnswers: A benefit for combining multiple Azure subscriptions
A discount for hybrid cloud deployments
A benefit for using Azure with other clouds

questions.question: What is Azure AD Connect?
questions.answer: Azure AD Connect synchronizes on-premises Active Directory with Azure AD. It supports password hash sync, pass-through authentication, and federation. Azure AD Connect Health monitors sync health and provides alerts for identity integration issues.
wrongAnswers: A tool for connecting Azure VMs to AD
A service for connecting Azure to other clouds
A database connector for Azure AD

questions.question: What is Azure AD B2B?
questions.answer: Azure AD B2B (Business-to-Business) enables collaboration with external partners. Guest users from other organizations can access your resources using their own credentials. B2B provides secure external sharing with conditional access and compliance policies.
wrongAnswers: A business-to-business network service
A database for storing B2B partner data
A tool for managing B2B contracts

questions.question: What is Azure AD B2C?
questions.answer: Azure AD B2C is an identity management service for consumer-facing applications. It provides sign-up, sign-in, and profile management for applications. B2C supports social identity providers, custom branding, and scales to millions of identities.
wrongAnswers: A business-to-consumer messaging service
A database for storing consumer data
A CDN service for B2C applications

questions.question: What is Azure Media Services?
questions.answer: Media Services provides broadcast quality video streaming and real-time broadcasting. It encodes, stores, and delivers video content at scale. Integrates with Content Delivery Network for global distribution. Supports live events and video-on-demand streaming.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing media storage
A database for storing media metadata
A tool for testing media playback

questions.question: What is Azure API Management?
questions.answer: API Management publishes, secures, and monitors APIs. It provides API gateway for request handling, developer portal for API documentation, and analytics for usage insights. APIM supports authentication (OAuth, JWT), rate limiting, and caching policies.
wrongAnswers: A service for managing API documentation
A database for storing API configurations
A tool for testing API endpoints

questions.question: What is Azure Functions consumption plan?
questions.answer: Consumption plan provides serverless billing where you pay only for execution time. It automatically scales based on load and includes free monthly grants. Limitations include maximum execution time (5-10 minutes) and cold start latency.
wrongAnswers: A plan for consuming Azure resources at a fixed rate
A premium plan with guaranteed resources
A plan for consuming Azure data storage

questions.question: What is Azure Durable Functions?
questions.answer: Durable Functions extends Azure Functions for stateful workflows. It enables long-running orchestrations, human interaction patterns, and complex branching. Orchestrator functions define workflow, activity functions perform tasks, and entity functions manage state.
wrongAnswers: Functions that persist data to durable storage
Functions with long-running execution times
Functions that can be paused and resumed

questions.question: What is Azure Blueprints?
questions.answer: Azure Blueprints enables repeatable, compliant infrastructure deployment. It composes ARM templates, RBAC assignments, and policy assignments into a single package. Blueprints can version and update existing deployments while maintaining compliance with organizational standards.
wrongAnswers: A service for creating Azure resource blueprints
A database for storing blueprint configurations
A tool for designing Azure architecture

questions.question: What is Azure Resource Graph?
questions.answer: Resource Graph enables fast, complex queries across all subscriptions and resource groups. It uses an optimized query engine to explore resource properties and relationships. Resource Graph powers Azure Portal's search and enables large-scale resource inventory management.
wrongAnswers: A service for creating resource diagrams
A database for storing resource configurations
A tool for graphing resource costs

questions.question: What are Azure best practices for security?
questions.answer: Security best practices include: use RBAC for least privilege access, enable Azure AD Conditional Access, implement network segmentation with VNets and NSGs, use Private Link for sensitive services, enable Azure Defender, encrypt data at rest and in transit, use Key Vault for secrets, enable monitoring with Azure Monitor, implement Azure Security Center recommendations, and regularly audit with Azure Policy.
wrongAnswers: Disable all security features for better performance
Use the same credentials for all resources
Store all data in public storage for easy access
